shape_semantics: Joint-necessity. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,L,W,L: ONLY value_profile_cmplog resolves; cmplog, value_profile, and naive all block. Every branch carries TWO Pass-A signatures of ONE joint mechanism: an h0 (vpc>value_profile, attributed to I2S => I2S necessary) and an h1 (vpc>cmplog, attributed to CMP_MAP gradient => value_profile necessary). The shape itself (Layer 1) already asserts both techniques are jointly required; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm jointness (that would violate G2 by verifying vpc-resolves with vpc-resolves). Instead it discriminates WHICH joint subtype each branch is, on a measurement INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern: it reads the structural/value content of the cmplog-BLOCKING and value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets and asks what the gradient adds OVER I2S-only (cmplog), and what I2S adds OVER gradient-only (value_profile).

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER, multi-field structure than I2S-only (cmplog) ever reaches; the gradient is necessary to grow size/token-count past where exact substitution stalls, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) places ~0 structural tokens
hypotheses.prediction: On the value_profile-BLOCKING seed set the structural-token count is ~0 (vp_tags ~ 0): the gradient alone cannot place ANY exact multi-byte structural token, so I2S is necessary. AND the vpc-RESOLVING median seed is materially BIGGER than the cmplog-BLOCKING median seed (size_lift >= 1.3): the gradient grows the structure past where exact-substitution-only (cmplog) plateaus, so the gradient is necessary. A pure-I2S-substitution mechanism (no assembly depth) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 (vpc and cmplog reach the SAME-sized structure, cmplog just hasn't placed the one tag yet); a pure-gradient mechanism would predict vp_tags > 0 (the gradient could place tokens on its own). Only the assembly-depth joint predicts BOTH vp_tags~0 AND size_lift>=1.3.

hypotheses.label: vpc and cmplog reach a SIMILARLY-sized structure (size_lift ~ 1, sometimes vpc smaller), but vpc places a specific gradient-climbed VALUE that the cmplog-blocking seeds approach yet never reach; I2S is still necessary to place the surrounding exact literal(s), and the gradient is necessary for the last-mile value precision the structural proxy is blind to

params.comment: Built tool value_distance_reached (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch). For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value (operand_literal from the signature, e.g. 0x00020000 for harfbuzz/5609-5616, the 'loca'/'post' tag for 5440/5442, the interned name3 string for libxml2/6414, the chained language/script tag for 6214/6223). The tool computes the MIN over each arm's seeds of (min over sliding windows of Hamming(window, target_operand)/width); numeric mode for inequality gates. vpc_min_distance is the vpc-RESOLVING arm, cmp_min_distance the cmplog-BLOCKING arm; distance_closure_ratio = (cmp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance)/max(cmp_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from the per-role coverage overlay or the analysis byte-diff (the W-vs-L divergent offset)
params.feasibility: BUILT + validated (anchor sqlite3/14437; 4/4 local anchors winner_closer=true). Seed sets for this hypothesis's harfbuzz + libxml2 members exist (286-rep DB); all scorable.

hypotheses.prediction: Structures are the SAME size (size_lift ~ 1.0, the assembly-depth proxy is blind here) BUT the vpc-resolving seeds reach a value strictly CLOSER to the target operand than the cmplog-blocking seeds ever do: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog seeds approach but never hit the exact value) while vpc_min_distance ~ 0 (vpc lands it), so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S mechanism would predict cmp_min_distance ~ 0 too (cmplog HAS I2S, so if a single substitution sufficed cmplog would also reach the value and resolve -- contradicting the L label); the fact that cmplog approaches-but-misses is exactly what the gradient (value_profile) supplies. This rules out 'I2S alone is enough' AND, because size_lift~1, rules out the assembly-depth story.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1). The cmplog-BLOCKING arm reaches a value that is STRICTLY CLOSER to the gate operand than naive/value_profile but still NON-zero, while the vpc-RESOLVING arm lands it (distance ~ 0): the value-profile CMP_MAP gradient supplies the last-mile closure on a fixed/string operand that cmplog's discrete I2S substitution stalls short of, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) never reaches the operand window. Same-size structures (the assembly-depth size proxy is blind). This is the value-closure subtype the prior joint_value_precision rule under-captured because it cross-referenced size_lift (a different tool's metric) and used too strict a closure floor; this hypothesis scores entirely on value_distance_reached's OWN validated metrics.

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool (descriptor_key seed_set:value_distance_reached:operand_literal:per_branch), scored ONLY on its own native metrics (no cross-tool size_lift dependency). Per-branch target operand from signature.operand_literal: harfbuzz/5609,5614,5616 -> 0x00020000 (width 4, derived_integer, numeric mode); lcms/2107 -> closest of {link,prtr,spac}; lcms/2184 -> closest of {link,abst}; lcms/2196 -> closest of {lams,link}; lcms/2209 -> closest of {lcms,link,prtr,spac,MCH1}; libxml2/7326 -> '<![CDATA[' (variable-length string, edit/prefix mode). All operands width>=4 so chance-alignment caveat (width<=2) does not apply.
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes; cmp-site offset from per-role overlay / analysis byte-diff (W-vs-L divergent offset, e.g. 0x18 version field for 5609/5614/5616, 0x0c device-class FOURCC for lcms).
params.feasibility: BUILT (value_distance_reached, validated). harfbuzz on-disk corpus on s4. lcms + libxml2 corpora are owned by server B (on-disk lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty); their resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds are in the 286-rep DB which is what the seed_set source consumes, so server B arbitrates them. Per cross-server rule these are NOT marked decidable:false for absence on s4.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-blocking arm gets CLOSER to the operand than the gradient-less baselines yet stalls at cmp_min_distance > 0; the vpc-resolving arm closes it (vpc_min_distance ~ 0), so winner_closer == true and distance_gap > 0 with a substantial distance_closure_ratio. Discriminating: a PURE-I2S story predicts cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog has I2S; one substitution would land the fixed 4-byte constant / FOURCC and resolve -- contradicting the L label), so a non-zero cmp_min_distance with winner_closer rules out 'I2S alone is enough'. A PURE-GRADIENT story predicts value_profile reaches the window too; but vp blocks, so I2S-placed surrounding bytes are required. A pure ASSEMBLY-DEPTH story predicts winner_closer driven by a bigger structure, not a closer value -- here the structures are same-size and the gap is in the operand window, not token count. Only value-gradient-closure predicts winner_closer + non-zero cmp_min_distance + a same-size structure.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.34

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 1, honest inconclusive). Joint gates whose discriminating content is a CATEGORY-SEQUENCE / range gradient or a multi-token STRUCTURAL-PRESENCE condition with NO single fixed operand value to measure distance to, AND for which the token-count assembly proxy already failed to fire -- no BUILT measurement can separate the I2S vs gradient contribution. Left honestly inconclusive (G3).

params.comment: harfbuzz/5980: USE-machine case-10 transition gated by a RANGE comparison _keys[0] <= use_category() <= _keys[1] over a category-code SEQUENCE; operand_kind=enum_or_code, operand_literal=null. There is no fixed target value for value_distance_reached, no structural-token vocabulary for joint_necessity (no sfnt/FOURCC tags involved -- it is a per-glyph category prefix), and depth_reach measures iteration count not category-sequence content. libxml2/8161: 'second xmlNewNs on the same node' -- presence_or_nonnull on both h0 and h1, operand_literal=null; it is a multi-token structural-well-formedness condition (intact <?xml/<!DOCTYPE/xmlns chain) that the KEPT joint_assembly_depth (token_count) rule already scored and did not hold (size_lift / vp_tags thresholds not met). A relaxed-threshold re-test of the same token_count measurement is forbidden, and no DIFFERENT built tool discriminates a structural-presence gate (value_distance has no operand; depth_reach measures depth not presence).
params.feasibility: NOT discriminable by any built tool. registry_tool null and decidable:false -- the arbiter leaves these two branches inconclusive rather than force-fit a mechanism.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable real-data prediction can be made that a built tool could score: 5980 has no fixed operand for a distance metric and no structural tokens for a count metric; 8161's only available measurement (token_count) already failed and cannot be relaxed. Honest decidable:false.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' is used (I2S is untagged in mutation_op).

No byte-alignment / byte_freq_ratio is used (joint gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds); value content is measured via value_distance_reached (operand-window Hamming/edit distance, alignment-free) and structure via struct_size_and_token_count.
value_profile is treated as a FEEDBACK (its effect read via what the corpus reaches: token count + value distance), never as a lineage gradient event.

Seed sets consumed from db/blockers.sqlite resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (the 286 reps have them). On-disk corpus is required only by operand_enrichment/depth_reach, neither of which this test uses; cross-server, lcms+libxml2 are arbitrated by server B which owns their corpora.
notes:
ROUND-1 RE-DESIGN (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET / FREEZE: both prior hypotheses (joint_assembly_depth, joint_value_precision) are carried forward VERBATIM (rules unchanged) so the 12 CONFIRMED branches (6x each) re-validate. Only NEW hypotheses were added, for the 11 UNLABELED branches.

PRIORS KEPT (2): joint_assembly_depth [vp_tags<0.3 AND size_lift>=1.3] and joint_value_precision [size_lift<1.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5] -- both unchanged.

NEW MECHANISM (1 decidable): joint_value_gradient_closure. Covers the 9 unlabeled value-gate branches whose discriminating content is a FIXED/STRING operand the cmplog arm approaches-but-misses: harfbuzz/5609,5614,5616 (exact 0x00020000 post-table version), lcms/2107,2184,2196,2209 (device-class/colorspace FOURCC), libxml2/7326 ('<![CDATA[' token). Why NEW and not a relaxed joint_value_precision: (a) NOVELTY/G1 -- it is a genuinely tighter mechanism story (last-mile value closure on a same-size structure) scored on value_distance_reached's OWN native metrics [winner_closer, distance_gap, distance_closure_ratio, cmp_min_distance] with NO cross-tool size_lift coupling; the prior rule mixed joint_necessity's size_lift with value-distance metrics, which is why it failed to score these. (b) Its rule is not a threshold-relaxation of an already-scored measurement on these branches -- the prior member_hint for value_precision did NOT include these lcms/libxml2/version branches, and the joint_assembly_depth measurement (the one that WAS scored on them) is a DIFFERENT compute (token_count, not value_distance). (c) DISCRIMINATING: winner_closer + non-zero cmp_min_distance rules out pure-I2S (cmplog has I2S; if a single substitution landed the fixed constant it would resolve, contradicting L), and same-size structure rules out assembly-depth.

HONEST decidable:false SUB-GROUP (2 branches): joint_sequence_or_structural_nondiscriminable = harfbuzz/5980 (USE category-RANGE/sequence gradient -- no fixed operand for value_distance, no structural tokens for token_count, depth_reach measures iteration not category content) + libxml2/8161 (two-namespace structural-PRESENCE, operand_literal=null on both halves -- the only applicable built measurement is token_count which the KEPT joint_assembly_depth rule already scored and did not hold; relaxing it is forbidden, and no DIFFERENT built tool discriminates a presence gate). Per G3 these are correctly left inconclusive rather than force-labeled.

DECISION ORDER: assembly_depth (most specific conjunction) -> value_precision (prior, strict closure>=0.5) -> value_gradient_closure (new, closure>=0.34 native-metric) -> nondiscriminable (decidable:false). A branch matching none falls through to fallback:inconclusive. CROSS-SERVER: harfbuzz on s4; lcms+libxml2 on server B (which owns their on-disk corpora); all seed-set tools consume the shared 286-rep DB seeds, so every branch is scorable by its owning server -- none is marked decidable:false merely for corpus absence on s4. OPERAND-WIDTH: every value_distance operand here is width>=4 (0x00020000, 4-byte FOURCCs, the 9-byte '<![CDATA[' string), so the tool's short-operand (width<=2) chance-alignment caveat does not apply.
